API definition for read-only SOVD data resources
- Can be safely shared across threads
- Multiple threads can call read() concurrently
- May fetch data from remote ECUs/services
- Prevents blocking threads during network calls
API definition for writable SOVD data resources
- Only has &mut self method (mutable exclusive access)
- Cannot be safely shared across threads
- Can be sent between threads but not accessed concurrently
- Rust ensures exclusive access for mutation
- No tokio dependency in application layer
- Clearer semantics than implicit async behavior
API definition for SOVD data resources
- Blanket impl for T: ReadOnlyDataResource + WritableDataResource
- Sync because &self methods can be called concurrently
- Rust ensures &mut self requires exclusive access
Pattern: Mixed immutable/mutable access → Send + Sync
- Has &mut self methods: execute(), resume(), reset(), stop(), handle()
- Query methods (info, status): ASYNC - Operations may be long-running (calls to ECUs/services)
- Control methods (execute, resume, reset, stop): SYNC NON-BLOCKING
* Binding layer polls via status() to check completion
- handle(): ASYNC - custom OEM operations may need async I/O
API definition for UDS Service 'Read Data by Identifier'
- No concurrent access to same service in practice
- Send allows moving collection between threads
- Interior mutability (Cell, RefCell) for stateful DIDs
API definition for UDS Service 'Write Data by Identifier'
- Only has &mut self method (mutable exclusive access)
- Cannot be safely shared across threads
- Can be sent between threads but not accessed concurrently
- Rust ensures exclusive access for mutation
Blanket impl for T: ReadDataByIdentifier + WriteDataByIdentifier
API definition for UDS DataIdentifier (Read + Write)
- Blanket impl for T: ReadDataByIdentifier + WriteDataByIdentifier
- UDS services don't require concurrent access
- Enables interior mutability for stateful services
Note: This is a marker trait with no additional methods.
- Even though request_results is &self, trait stored as mutable
- Cannot be safely shared across threads
Design Choice: Treat as mutable resource despite one &self method
API definition for a generic UDS Service
- Cannot be safely shared across threads
- Can be sent between threads but not accessed concurrently
API definition for handling write operations
- Single trait implementation vs multiple closures
API definition for handling routine control operations
- Single trait object vs 3 separate closures
by serializing T into UDS byte format
- Send: Required for moving into Box and across threads
- 'static: Required for type erasure (no borrowed data)
- Sync: NOT required (UDS binding is single-threaded)
Removing Sync allows T to use Cell, RefCell, etc.
Example: Tracking read counts, caching, dynamic state
by deserializing UDS bytes into T and calling handler
Now uses WriteHandler<T> trait instead of closure.
- No Box allocation if H is concrete type
- Can Box if needed: SerializedWriteDataByIdentifier<T, Box<dyn WriteHandler<T>>>
- More flexible: handler can have mutable state
Now uses WriteHandler<T> trait instead of closure.
- Send: Required for type erasure into Box
- 'static: Required for type erasure (no borrowed data)
